mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and high consumer confidence, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero average forward return over 60 trading days in backtests, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum trading and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 50% across all historical recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.6% threshold, invalidating the inflation-aversion mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings capacity and economic output, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month contraction in industrial production signals severe supply-side weakness and impending recession, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for three consecutive months
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of periods following a three-month industrial production decline, contradicting the recession-aversion mechanism.
